13.reason: // The condition should check for EOF; fgetc returns int, not char, and loop never terminates properly because '\0' is not EOF.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [The loop condition is incorrect: fgetc() returns EOF on end-of-file or error, which is typically -1. The condition should be (c = fgetc(in)) != EOF. As written, the loop stops when c is 0 (null byte), but text files may not contain null bytes, causing infinite loop or undefined behavior. Also, if file fails to open, in is NULL and fgetc causes crash.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  // The condition should check for EOF; fgetc returns int, not char, and loop never terminates properly because '\0' is not EOF.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [The loop condition is incorrect: fgetc() returns EOF on end-of-file, which is typically -1, not 0. The loop should check for != EOF. Also, if the file fails to open, in is NULL and fgetc(in) causes undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  // The loop condition is incorrect: fgetc returns EOF on end-of-file or error, which is typically -1. The condition should be (c = fgetc(in)) != EOF. As written, when fgetc returns 0 (which can happen if the file contains a null byte), the loop stops prematurely. Also, if the file is empty or cannot be opened, in is NULL and fgetc causes undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [The loop condition is incorrect: fgetc() returns EOF on end-of-file or error, which is typically -1. The condition should be 'while((c = fgetc(in)) != EOF)' and c should be declared as int to properly handle EOF. Also, using char for c may cause infinite loop or undefined behavior when EOF is returned.]

15.reason: // Redundant: c is already lowercase; also, tolower should be applied to uppercase letters.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Redundant: c is already lowercase if between 'a' and 'z'. Also, uppercase letters are not handled because this condition skips them. Should convert uppercase to lowercase before checking word boundaries.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  // Redundant: c is already lowercase; also, tolower should be applied to uppercase letters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Redundant: c is already lowercase if it's between 'a' and 'z'. Also, uppercase letters are not handled because the condition skips them.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  // Redundant: c is already lowercase if it's between 'a' and 'z'. Also, tolower should be applied to uppercase letters, not lowercase.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Redundant: c is already lowercase if it's between 'a' and 'z'. Also, tolower() should be applied to uppercase letters, not lowercase. Moreover, the logic misses converting uppercase letters because the condition only checks for lowercase.]

45.reason: // Loop runs up to 30, but word length is stored in d[i]; should only compare up to d[i] characters.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Loop bound 30 may exceed actual word length stored in d[i]; should use d[i] instead to avoid comparing garbage data.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  // Loop runs up to 30, but word length is stored in d[i]; should only compare up to d[i] characters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop runs up to 30, but word length is stored in d[i], which may be less than 30. This can compare uninitialized/garbage characters beyond the actual word length.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  // Loop runs up to 30, but words may be shorter than 30; comparing beyond d[i] (word length) leads to comparing uninitialized/garbage values.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Loop bound 30 may exceed actual word length stored in d[i], leading to comparison of uninitialized/garbage characters. Should loop up to d[i] instead.]

77.reason: // Again, comparing up to fixed 30 instead of actual word length; may read uninitialized memory.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Again, using fixed bound 30 instead of actual word length d[i] or d[j] may compare uninitialized memory, leading to incorrect lexicographical comparison.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  // Again, comparing up to fixed 30 instead of actual word length; may read uninitialized memory.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Same issue: comparing up to 30 characters regardless of actual word length d[i] or d[j], leading to incorrect lexicographical comparison.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  // Again, comparing up to 30 characters regardless of actual word length d[i] or d[j], leading to incorrect lexicographical comparison.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Again, using fixed bound 30 instead of actual word length (d[i] or d[j]) may compare garbage data beyond the word, causing incorrect lexicographical ordering.]

79.reason: [Swapping entire 30-char arrays even though words may be shorter; inefficient but not logically wrong if unused chars are zero. However, d[] array swap below has a bug.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Swapping entire 30-char arrays even though words may be shorter; inefficient but not logically wrong if unused chars are zero. However, d[] array swap below has a bug.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  // Swapping entire 30-character arrays even if words are shorter; also, d[i] and d[j] are swapped incorrectly below.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Swapping entire 30-character arrays regardless of actual word length wastes time and may swap garbage; should only swap up to max(d[i], d[j]) or use strcpy.]

86.reason: // Bug: d[j] is overwritten before being saved; should swap using temp variable correctly.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Bug: d[j] is assigned d[i] after d[i] was overwritten. Should be d[j] = p; to complete the swap. This corrupts word lengths used in printing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  // Bug: d[j] is overwritten before being saved; should swap using temp variable correctly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Bug: d[j] is assigned d[i] after d[i] was overwritten; should swap using temp variable. This sets both d[i] and d[j] to original d[i].]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  // Bug: d[j] is assigned d[i] after d[i] was overwritten. Should be d[j] = p;
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Bug: d[j] is assigned d[i] after d[i] was overwritten. Should store original d[i] in temp variable. This causes loss of original d[j] value.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This check is inside the t-loop, so it may trigger prematurely before full word comparison. It should be after the loop.]
